claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Is Alex your biological child, adopted child, or do you have a different relationship with them (such as being a foster parent or another arrangement)?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I have a couple more questions about your circumstances.

Are you responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, such as paying for their food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am responsible for Alex's day-to-day care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Are you currently living in the United Kingdom?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more question:

Is Alex in full-time education or any approved training, and are they currently living with you full-time (i.e., not in local authority care or living with someone else for an extended period)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 8, so yes, they're in primary school. And yes, they live with me full-time - they're not in care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just check one final thing:

What is your immigration status? Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled or pre-settled status, or another type of visa or immigration status?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all the information you've provided. Based on the details you've shared and the Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now assess your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

**For Alex (age 8):**
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✅ Alex lives with you full-time
- ✅ You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- ✅ You are a British citizen living in the UK
- ✅ Alex is in full-time education and not in local authority care

You meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. You can proceed to make a claim through the official government channels.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant is Alex's biological parent, lives with Alex full-time, and is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care (food, clothes, accommodation). The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK. Alex is in full-time education (primary school), is not in local authority care, and lives with the claimant full-time. All the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met: the claimant is responsible for a child under 16 and lives in the UK.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK (Manchester) who is responsible for their 8-year-old biological child, Alex. Alex lives with the claimant full-time, is in primary school, and is not in local authority care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

The claimant is Alex's biological parent, lives with Alex full-time, and is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care (food, clothes, accommodation). The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK. Alex is in full-time education (primary school), is not in local authority care, and lives with the claimant full-time. All the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met: the claimant is responsible for a child under 16 and lives in the UK.

Alex lives with the claimant full-time, is in primary school, and is not in local authority care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
